title: Calling C API in verilator: Unsupported DPI argument of type REFDTYPE
body:
Hi there,

I realize this is being called out as an unsupported feature. I am basically trying to pass a C function with function prototype like:

```c
// Example C prototype corresponding to the DPI-C import task
int example_dpi_task(
    custom_struct_t* cfg,       // equivalent to 'input custom_struct_t cfg' in SV
    uint32_t channel,           // 'input int unsigned channel'
    int32_t mode_select         // 'input int mode_select'
);
```

And I call it in SV, but I get the below error (when doing a DPI-C import):
```
%Error-UNSUPPORTED: src/top/testbench.sv:102:65: Unsupported: DPI argument of type REFDTYPE 'custom_struct_t'
                                                    : ... For best portability, use bit, byte, int, or longint
  102 | import "DPI-C" context task example_dpi_task(input custom_struct_t cfg, input int unsigned channel, input int mode_select);
      |                                                                 ^~~
                     src/top/testbench.sv:15:1: ... note: In file included from 'testbench.sv'
                     ... For error description see https://verilator.org/warn/UNSUPPORTED?v=5.040
```

I am happy to try and take a crack at implementing this if I can get some guidance on where to start/how this could be supported.
